perturbation.original_text: The parties may disclose the Confidential Information to their respective officers, directors, employees, attorneys, subsidiaries and affiliates working in connection with the purpose for which the Confidential Information is disclosed but only to the extent necessary to carry out that purpose and subject to all requirements of confidentiality set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties may disclose the Confidential Information to their respective officers, directors, employees, attorneys, subsidiaries and affiliates working in connection with the purpose for which the Confidential Information is disclosed, without any limitations.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the limitation that disclosure should only be 'to the extent necessary' and 'subject to all requirements of confidentiality,' creating a contradiction. It now suggests unrestricted disclosure, conflicting with other clauses that emphasize the importance of confidentiality. This makes it difficult to enforce the confidentiality provisions, as one section allows unlimited disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein and agrees further that the Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose nor disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes limitations on disclosing Confidential Information, specifically removing 'to the extent necessary' and 'subject to all requirements of confidentiality'. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that Confidential Information should be held in confidence, used solely for the purposes contemplated, and not disclosed to any third party without prior written consent. The changed text now allows for unlimited disclosure, whereas the contradicted text strictly limits disclosure. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall not reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile, or otherwise analyze, restructure and/or copy or in any way recreate in whole or in part any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party may reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile, or otherwise analyze, restructure and/or copy or in any way recreate in whole or in part any Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, provided such actions are done for legitimate business purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces a significant contradiction by allowing reverse engineering, disassembly, and decompilation, which were previously prohibited. This conflicts with the intent to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information. The phrase 'legitimate business purposes' is vague, leading to disputes about what constitutes a legitimate purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Subject only to the Receiving Party's limited use of the Confidential Information for the purpose set forth above in this Agreement, the Receiving Party acknowledges and agrees that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights, license or otherwise, to any Confidential Information disclosed pursuant to this Agreement, and the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property or other rights in or to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited reverse engineering, ensuring the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information remained protected. The changed text permits reverse engineering for 'legitimate business purposes,' directly contradicting the Contradicted Text, which states that the Receiving Party shall not violate any of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property or other rights in or to the Confidential Information and that nothing in the agreement grants any rights to the Confidential Information beyond the limited use initially defined. This creates an inconsistency, as the Receiving Party is now granted the ability to reverse engineer, which could lead to a violation of the Disclosing Party's intellectual property rights. The ambiguity of 'legitimate business purposes' further exacerbates this contradiction, making the enforceability of the intellectual property protection uncertain.

perturbation.original_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information is and shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: All right, title and interest in and to the Confidential Information and intellectual property produced based on the Confidential Information shall be jointly owned by the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'sole property' to 'jointly owned' creates a direct contradiction. This contradicts the purpose of the agreement to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information, raising disputes over intellectual property rights and ownership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein and agrees further that the Confidential Information shall not be used for any other purpose nor disclosed to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party. Each party agrees it shall apply the same level of confidential treatment to the other party's Confidential Information as it does to its own Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the Disclosing Party retains 'sole property' of the confidential information and intellectual property. The changed text alters this to 'jointly owned by the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party'. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'Each party agrees to hold the other's Confidential Information in confidence and to use it solely for the purposes contemplated herein'. The change in ownership significantly impacts the confidentiality agreement, creating a clear conflict and inconsistency, as now the Receiving Party has ownership rights, undermining the sole use provision.

perturbation.original_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via registered or certified mail, postage prepaid, return receipt requested; by facsimile transmission, with confirmation of receipt; or by overnight courier service to the other party as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via email to the other party as follows:
perturbation.explanation: The modified text contradicts the original method of communication specified (registered/certified mail, fax, courier) by stating only 'email'. This affects the enforceability of notices and other communications, potentially invalidating them if not sent via the original methods. This creates an in-text contradiction because the change directly conflicts with the specific methods and addresses provided in the subsequent lines of the same section.

Any notice or other communication made or given by either party in connection with this Agreement shall be sent via email to the other party as follows:

Any party may by notice as set forth in this paragraph change the address to which further notices to it shall be mailed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the allowed communication method to only email. The contradicted text provides physical addresses and fax numbers, which are rendered useless if email is the only accepted method. This creates a clear contradiction because the specified contact information becomes irrelevant. The explanation confirms that enforceability of notices is affected, potentially invalidating them if not sent via the original methods.
